title: URL Allowlisting, Untrusted API Data, Terminal Safety, Files, and Resource Abuse · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent conservation group receives a different region, command sequence, API fixture, cache age, concurrency budget, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers url allowlisting, untrusted api data, terminal safety, files, and resource abuse into a changed deny-by-default Pokedex trust-boundary policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of deny-by-default Pokedex trust-boundary policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progr…
objectives: Revalidate scheme, host, port, redirect count, and destination policy on every hop and reject user-controlled egress.
Normalize or visibly escape control, bidi, and ANSI sequences from API, file, and user data before terminal presentation.
Choose application-owned storage roots, reject traversal or unexpected file types, and minimize permission and symlink assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Pokedex Go Redirect Egress Pol…
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 3996 during a fair-use API stewardship review. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce destination, terminal, file, privacy, and resource trust boundaries; browser code must remain deterministic and side-effect-free; then change one resource identity, pagination link, JSON field, status, or body budget and revalidate admission. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
